name: Ikokore
intro: A staple meal of the Ijebu people from the Yoruba tribe, Ikokore is similar to Asaro (page 54) but makes use of water yam, a variety of yam that is slimy and slippery in texture. The water yam is grated or blended before cooking, producing a melt- in-the-mouth porridge. The traditional recipe calls for stockfish and crayfish to boost the flavour, but these can be replaced with dried seaweed and smoky jackfruit.

ingredients.item: medium water yam (approx. 1kg) (peeled and cut into large chunks)
jackfruit or other pre- cooked meat substitute of you choice

method: Place the red bell peppers, onion, scotch bonnet chilli and 1 cup of water in a food blender and blend until smooth.

Heat the palm oil in a pot and add the blended peppers/ onion mixture. Cover the pot partially and cook on high heat for 10 minutes. Add 1 and 1/4 cups of water, cayenne pepper, stock cube, iru and salt. Mix well to combine.
Add the grated water yam into the stew in big and small dollops around the pot. Do not stir. Cover and cook for 15 minutes over medium heat.
To make smoky jackfruit, toss with smoked paprika, sunflower oil and salt, and bake in the oven for 20 minutes at 200°C.
